title: A Thing of Beauty by John Keats
subject: English

notes.heading: Theme
notes.text: Beauty is eternal and gives us hope. In a world full of pain and evil, beauty in nature and art heals us and gives meaning to life.

notes.heading: Key Literary Devices
points: Metaphor: “endless fountain of immortal drink”, “bower quiet”
Imagery: sun, moon, trees, daffodils, streams, and musk-roses

summary:
This poem is an excerpt from Keats’ long poem Endymion. Keats says a beautiful thing gives us permanent joy: “A thing of beauty is a joy forever.” Its loveliness never fades. Instead, it increases over time and never passes into nothingness. A beautiful thing is like a shady shelter that gives us sleep full of sweet dreams, health, and quiet breathing.

Life is full of sadness, gloom, and despair. There are trials, difficulties, and a lack of noble people. We face dark, unhealthy ways and overcast days. Despite all this, some beautiful things remove the pall of sadness from our spirits. They make life worth living.

Keats lists what he considers beautiful: the sun, the moon, old and young trees that provide shade, daffodils with their green world, clear streams that cool us in hot seasons, and the mid-forest brake rich with musk-roses. Also beautiful are the lovely tales we have heard or read, such as stories of the mighty dead.

All these are an endless fountain of immortal drink pouring from heaven. They give us endless joy and strength to face life’s sorrows. Beauty never dies. It connects us to nature, history, and imagination, and keeps our spirits alive.

qa.q: List the things of beauty mentioned in the poem.
qa.a: The things of beauty mentioned in the poem are the sun, the moon, old and young trees, daffodils, clear streams, thick forest ferns, musk-rose flowers, and lovely stories of great people.

qa.q: List the things that cause suffering and pain.
qa.a: The things that cause suffering and pain are sadness, lack of noble people, gloomy days, unhealthy ways, and dark thoughts.

qa.q: What does the line, “Therefore are we wreathing a flowery band to bind us to earth” suggest to you?
qa.a: This line suggests that beautiful things connect us to life and give us hope. Beauty helps us continue living despite pain and sadness.

qa.q: What makes human beings love life in spite of troubles and sufferings?
qa.a: Beautiful things in nature and noble stories make human beings love life. They remove sadness from our minds and give us peace, joy, and hope.

qa.q: Why is ‘grandeur’ associated with the ‘mighty dead’?
qa.a: ‘Grandeur’ is associated with the ‘mighty dead’ because great people are remembered with respect and admiration. Their noble deeds and inspiring stories continue to live after their death.

qa.q: Do we experience things of beauty only for short moments or do they make a lasting impression on us?
qa.a: Things of beauty make a lasting impression on us. Their joy never disappears; instead, their loveliness increases with time.

qa.q: What image does the poet use to describe the beautiful bounty of the earth?
qa.a: The poet describes the beautiful bounty of the earth as an “endless fountain of immortal drink” pouring down from heaven. It means beauty is a never-ending source of joy and peace.
